classes.description: After interaction with level L from below or at L, price establishes meaningfully above L within the decision horizon: sustained closes or structural holds above L per Step 6 numeric rules; immediate collapse back below without persistence invalidates.
classes.semantic_intent: Bullish acceptance of resistance-turned-support or breakout hold above L.
positive_conditions: Price clears L to the upside with minimum persistence (e.g. N closes above L within horizon).
Retest holds above L rather than full round-trip failure.
negative_conditions: Price returns and holds below L before horizon end without prior persistence.
Only a wick pierce with no close-based acceptance.
common_confusions: false_breakout_up when price pierces L then fails back below within horizon
rejection when price never establishes above L
invalidated_by: ambiguous anchor or undefined L at T0
classes.notes: Mirror semantics for below-level acceptance is acceptance_below.

classes.description: Mirror of acceptance_above: price establishes meaningfully below L within horizon when approach is from above or at L, with persistence; quick reclaim above L invalidates.
classes.semantic_intent: Bearish acceptance / breakdown hold below L.
positive_conditions: Sustained closes below L within horizon per Step 6 thresholds.
negative_conditions: Immediate reclaim and hold above L without persistence below.

classes.description: Price breaks above L (pierce or close) but fails acceptance: within decision_horizon returns and holds below L without meeting acceptance_above persistence rules.
classes.semantic_intent: Liquidity grab or stop-run above L that fails.
positive_conditions: Pierce or short-lived trade above L followed by failure back below L within horizon.
negative_conditions: Meets acceptance_above persistence — then label acceptance_above, not false breakout.
common_confusions: rejection if no meaningful pierce occurred
classes.notes: Evaluated before acceptance in class_decision_order when both could be argued; order resolves to false_breakout first.

classes.description: Mirror of false_breakout_up for downside pierce below L that fails and reclaims above L within horizon.
positive_conditions: Pierce below L then failure back above L within horizon without acceptance_below persistence.

classes.description: Price tests L (touches or near-touch within tolerance) and moves away without satisfying false_breakout or acceptance classes: classic bounce or fade without breakout hold or failed-breakout structure.
classes.semantic_intent: Level holds; counter-move without setup completion in other classes.
positive_conditions: Clear test of L and directional move away within horizon without persistence through L.
negative_conditions: Qualifies as acceptance_* or false_breakout_* under Step 6 thresholds.
common_confusions: no_setup when interaction is too weak to call a test
ambiguous when noise prevents telling test from chop

classes.description: The window is valid for the task (preconditions met) but no defined pattern for acceptance, false breakout, or rejection is present: chop inside band, no meaningful test, or structure outside v1 pattern catalog.
classes.semantic_intent: Negative space — task applicable, label negative for target patterns.
positive_conditions: OHLCV valid; anchor exists; none of the other classes' definitions are met.
negative_conditions: Another class is clearly satisfied — must not use no_setup.
Do not use no_setup when the correct withhold is ambiguous (use ambiguous).
common_confusions: ambiguous — ambiguous is for epistemic uncertainty, not mere absence of pattern
classes.notes: Fallback last in class_decision_order after all pattern checks fail.

classes.description: Withhold deterministic class: multiple classes remain plausible under written rules, evidence is insufficient, anchor/level geometry is unclear at T0, or microstructure noise exceeds Step 6 denoise limits. Not a catch-all for 'hard examples' when one class actually fits.
classes.semantic_intent: Explicit non-decision for training gold targets.
positive_conditions: Documented ambiguity_reason from enumerated codes in Step 6 (e.g. MULTIPLE_PLAUSIBLE, ANCHOR_UNCLEAR, INSUFFICIENT_BARS, EXCESSIVE_NOISE).
negative_conditions: Single clear class assignment possible — must assign that class or no_setup as appropriate.
Simply no pattern matched — use no_setup, not ambiguous.
common_confusions: weak confidence tier — tier is orthogonal; ambiguous is a class
classes.notes: gold is disallowed for ambiguous: withholding cannot be highest deterministic confidence.
